observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall submit to the Audit Team the breakdown of expenditures that was charged to Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses during the current year, in compliance with Paragraph 106 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and Section 61 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the “Government Auditing Code of the Philippines”.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE, faithfully:
a. Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and
submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January
31, of each year.
b. Require the General Services Officer to maintain and update
his property cards per category of PPE, pursuant to Section
119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update his
property ledger cards per category of PPE, pursuant to Section
120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant reconcile their property cards and property ledger
cards, respectively.

We recommended that the GSO to facilitate the issuance of the
accompanying PAR of the properties, pursuant to Section 123,
Volume I of the MNGAS, for LGUs.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Appropriately disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the
details of the Loans Payable, pursuant to paragraph
127(a)(b)(c) of IPSAS No. 1, and
b. Reclassify loan amortizations due for the next twelve months to
current liabilities and non-current liabilities for loan which is
due more than twelve months, pursuant to Section 80 of the
IPSAS 1 – Presentation of Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:
a. Confirm the ownership of the Projects as the Implementing
Agency;
b. Request the DPWH – Region 12 and MPW – BARMM, as Source
Agency for a proper turn-over ceremony of the Projects; and
c. Record the Assets and depreciation in the books of accounts of the
Municipality after the acceptance and proper turnover in
compliance to IPSAS 17 – Property Plant and Equipment.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reclassify the PPE accounts below ₱50,000.00 threshold to its appropriate
semi-expendable properties and expenses; and
b. Adjust the opening balance in the Statement of Changes in Net
Assets/Equity in compliance to the COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated
May 31, 2022.

We recommended that the General Services Officer facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying ICS of the semi-expendable properties,
pursuant to Section 4.1 of the COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated May
31, 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to record the appropriate accounts. It is
further recommended that future transactions be analyzed and
classified as to the prescribed accounts in Section 1(2) of the COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in order to avoid
misstatements and to fairly present the balances in the financial
statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant and other agency officials to transfer the prior year’s
unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE
to a Special Trust Fund account, Trust Liability-DRRM to ensure that
they shall be utilized only for the purpose of supporting disaster risk
reduction and management activities as required by laws, rules and
regulations.

Therefore, we highly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): Shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance with Section 74 of PD 1445, and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend to the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the issuance of the accompanying Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) of the properties, pursuant to Section 123, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommend that the General Services Officer post bond equal to the estimated amount of property under his possession, control and custody, respectively, in compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019.

We recommend that management should exercise prudence in incurring expenses and appropriately identify these expenses using the correct accounts in Volume III of the MNGAS for LGUs. Refrain from disbursing funds not appropriated for Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses but rather provide funds for other accounts.
We recommended that the Management recognize the Road Networks to the books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive consider creating and organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with Section 1 of the Administrative Order 70 and Section 2 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 3456 as amended by Republic Act 4177 to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.

Therefore, we highly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation
that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank
Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance
with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend the Management to:
a. Instruct the GSO to keep and maintain Stock Cards for each and
every supply to have an adequate monitoring and immediate
information of supplies on hand;
b. Require the Accounting Division to keep and maintain Supplies
Ledger Cards to record supplies received, issued and the balance
both in quantity and amount;
c. Ensure proper coordination between GSO and Accounting
Division to have an updated and reconciled balances in their Stock
Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively; and
d. Prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in compliance with Section
121 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for
Local Government Units, Volume I.

Due to the foregoing lapses noted, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of
supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same
as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the
appropriate ledgers;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the
necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards; and
c) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the
ledgers on a regular basis;

We recommend to the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying Property Acknowledgement Receipt
(PAR) of the properties, pursuant to Section 123, Volume I of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for
Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall submit to the
Audit Team the breakdown of expenditures that was charged to Other
Maintenance and Operating Expenses during the current year, in compliance with Paragraph 106 of the International Public
Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and Section 61 of Presidential
Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the “Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines”.

We recommend to require the General Services Officer to post bond
equal to the estimated amount of property under his possession,
control and custody, respectively, in compliance with Treasury
Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25,2019.

We recommend that management should exercise prudence in
incurring expenses and appropriately identify these expenses using
the correct accounts in Volume III of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Units (LGUs). Refrain from disbursing funds not appropriated for
Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses but rather provide funds
for other accounts.

The Head of Agency shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

1. Require the Municipal Accountant to appropriately disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of Philippines Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 1
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify loan amortizations due for the next twelve months to the current liabilities, and non-current liabilities for which is due more than twelve months pursuant to Paragraph 60 of the International Accounting Standards (IAS) No. 1.

1. Designate an employee who will be assigned to prepare, monitor and submit the required Monthly Fuel Consumption Report; and
2. Ensure that the forms, records, and reports prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61, on the reporting and control over fuel consumptions, are prepared, submitted and properly accomplished so wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures are minimized and disbursements are effectively controlled and properly accounted for.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Reports of Collections and Deposits for the calendar year 2020 together with the copies of the official receipt and validated deposit slips to support the total collections of revenues presented in the financial statements for the end of the year.

We recommended the management to direct all Revenue Collector to prepare RAAF while the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Consolidated RAAF and submit to the Unit Auditor the said monthly reports to the prescribed form provided in the Manual on New Government Accounting System not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month, pursuant to the Sections 50 and 51 of the said Manual.

1. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 37 - Disbursements, Section 40 - Payment by Check and Section 45 - Payments in Cash, of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).
2. Ensure that checks are enough or requested in advance to avoid problems regarding checks for the future transactions.

The Municipal accountant must set-up a receivable account for its expected revenue collection for a given year. The assessment on the real property taxes collections from the Office of the Municipal Assessor serves as basis of the records income as receivable for a year. The budget officer should furnish a copy of the budget for the year to the municipal accountant for setting-up receivable.
We recommended that the Management recognize the Road Networks to the books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.reason: Files from the previous accountants about the matter at hand is not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
1.) We recommend that the management strictly enforce the provisions pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support their daily needs until the next payday.
2.) We also recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of P5,000.00 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 48 of the 2019 GAA.
3.) Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee’s net pay fall below P5,000.00.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive consider creating and organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with Section 1 of the Administrative Order 70 and Section 2 of the Republic Act No. 3456 as amended by Republic Act 4177, to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The establishment of the IAS is on going.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal accountant must set-up a receivable account for its expected revenue collection for a given year. The budget officer should furnish a copy of the budget for the year to the municipal accountant for setting-up receivable.

observations.recommendation: 1.) We recommend that Management strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual. 2.) It is also advisable to have a stockroom provided for the inventory items of the agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and to prevent unauthorized use. 3.) The accountant should stop the practice of Inventory recording/charging regular purchases of supplies and materials immediately to the expense accounts and should maintain on a current basis in the General Ledger (GL) and Inventory Account for all purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period. Issuance thereof shall be recorded based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued.
observations.managementAction: The accountant stopped the practice of recording/charging regular purchases of supplies and maintained Account for all purchases of supplies for stock.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
1.) We recommend to the management to compute and record the
depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment. Compute the depreciation
using straight line method and depreciate it using the table of estimated
useful life as provided under COA Circular 2017-004.
Below is the suggested pro forma adjusting entry:
Prior Year’s Adjustment xxx
Accumulated Depreciation-Asset Account xxx

Recommendations:
(a) We recommended that the Management recognize the Road Networks to the
books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

Suggested Accounting Entries for the recognition of Road and Roadwork
in the Books of Accounts:
Account Title Account Code Debit Credit
Road Network 1-07-03-010 xxxxx
Government Equity xxxxx

Recommendations:
1.) We recommend that Management strictly observe the perpetual inventory
method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual.
2.) It is also advisable to have a stockroom provided for the inventory items of the
agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized use.
3.) The accountant should stop the practice of recording/charging regular
purchases of supplies and materials immediately to the expense accounts and
should maintain on a current basis in the General Ledger (GL) and Inventory
Account for all purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of
whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period. Issuance
thereof shall be recorded based on the Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued.

Recommendations:
1.) Maximize its authority and power to collect taxes and other charges
2.) Management should intensify its strategies and/or utilize other workable and
feasible approaches to prevent the non-collection of the Local Sources
Revenue for it could be an additional income which could have been used to
finance its programs/projects/activities.

Recommendation:
1.) We recommend that the management strictly enforce the provisions stipulated
in the 2018 GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and
effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough
to support their daily needs until the next payday.
2.) We also recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the
municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are
able to receive a minimum of P5,000.00 take home pay per month in
pursuance of Section 48 of the 2018 GAA.
3.) Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to
monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of
loans should an employee’s net pay fall below P5,000.00.

The municipal accountant should make an adjustment on the accounts stated and effect the correction on the books of accounts.

The management must submit RCI and RD with all the originals of vouchers/payrolls, contracts, accomplishment reports of completion and all supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. Failure of the management to submit the same shall be a ground of any appropriate action against the responsible officials.

All funds received as trust for LGU’s implementation shall be recorded as TRUST LIABILITY and not income and all expenses, including bank charges, related thereto are capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. Depreciation should not be recognized in this case.
Reconcile those variances in a statement of bank reconciliation and submit the same to the undersigned.
